stories.headline: FALLEN STAR: THE MANAGERS -- An Alchemist Who Turned Gold Into Lead; Financial Wizard Done In By His Smoke and Mirrors
stories.summary: A private hedge fund called Long-Term Capital Management, founded by John W Meriwether, is at the center of a $90 billion financial crisis that led to a $3.5 billion bailout. The bailout was orchestrated by the Federal Reserve Bank of New York and gave the participating banks control of 90 percent of Meriwether's company.

stories.headline: The Nation: Shadow Play -- Washington's Gears, Grinding; Surprise: Government's Not Necessarily Paralyzed
stories.summary: Despite the government's focus on the President's problems, the machinery of government and policy making has continued to function, but Congressional moves towards impeachment may hinder efforts to address major issues. Some officials are finding it difficult to get the attention of the President or his top aides.

stories.headline: Sports of The Times: A Sport Is Reborn -- The Home Run, America's Signature Feat, Invigorates Baseball; September's Aura Restored to Fans
stories.summary: The pursuit of the single-season home run record by Mark McGwire and Sammy Sosa has revitalized interest in baseball, with the two players closing in on Roger Maris's record of 61 home runs.

stories.headline: When Lending Money Just Isn't Enough; Critics Seek a New Role for the I.M.F. As Recent Bailout Programs Fall Short
stories.summary: The International Monetary Fund is facing criticism and calls for reform as its recent bailout programs have been unsuccessful, depleting its reserves due to costly bailouts of countries affected by the Asian crisis and Russia.

reveal_image.title: Biographical Directory of the United States Congress

rounds.blurb: The Long-Term Capital Management crisis unfolded, prompting a $3.5 billion bailout. The Federal Reserve Bank of New York orchestrated the rescue, gaining control of the company. Meanwhile, Mark McGwire and Sammy Sosa pursued the home run record.

stories.headline: SAME OLD SUGAR SENATE; Stands Doggedly by the Trust and Its Traditions. VOTES ON TARIFF AND CLOSURE The "Pop-Gun" Sugar Bill Practically Killed -- Vest's Motion for a Change of Rules Defeated. SAME OLD SUGAR SENATE
stories.summary: The Senate defeated a motion to consider a bill, and also rejected a proposal to change its rules. This decision effectively killed a sugar bill that was being considered.

stories.headline: BOND SYNDICATE ENDED; Sold One-fifth of the Fives, but Must Keep, Pro Rata, $19,500,000. MARKET DID NOT EXHIBIT EAGERNESS Bankers Talk of New Threes Against Two-Sevens and of a Better Market for the Fives Shortly.
stories.summary: A bond syndicate has been dissolved after selling a portion of its bonds, but the members must still hold a significant amount of them. The syndicate's dissolution was accompanied by criticism of the government's handling of the situation.

stories.headline: ANOTHER MAN CONFESSES; Indicted Policeman Thorne Told Mr. Lindsay Everything. WHAT IT WAS NOT YET DIVULGED The Assistant District Attorney Says, However, That the Revelations Are Astounding -- To Go to the Grand Jury.
stories.summary: A man has confessed to wrongdoing, revealing surprising information that will be presented to a grand jury. The confession was made to a district attorney and is related to a case involving a suspended policeman.

stories.headline: WILL PAY ITS DIVIDEND; After All the Talk About the Sugar Industry Being Ruined. THE SUGAR TRUST'S DEVIOUS WAYS Speculators Had a Rare Chance for a Quick Turn -- Sales Reached 170,482 Shares -- Mr. Have- meyer's Statements.
stories.summary: The Sugar Trust will pay its dividend despite concerns about the state of the sugar industry. The company's president made a statement about the dividend, and there was significant activity in the stock market as a result.

rounds.blurb: The Senate defeated a motion to consider a sugar bill, effectively killing it. The bond syndicate was dissolved after selling a portion of its bonds. A man confessed to wrongdoing, revealing surprising information to a district attorney.

stories.headline: WHITE HOUSE SAYS G. O. P. TOLD 'FACTS,' DENIES ANY 'SMEAR'; Democratic Leaders' Charge of 'Attacks' on Their Party Is Rejected by Hagerty PARTISAN TENSION GROWS Johnson Sides With Rayburn in Warning Administration Its Program Could Fail White House Rejects 'Smear' Cry; Says It 'Just Gave People Facts'
stories.summary: The White House denied that it was trying to smear the Democratic Party, saying it was just presenting facts. The administration also rejected claims that it was hindering New York City's ability to function.

stories.headline: MOLOTOV BLOCKS AN AUSTRIAN PACT BY OLD PROVISOS; Accepts Move to Finish Treaty Before Talks End Thursday, but Demands His Changes DULLES CHARGES 'FRAUD' Asserts U.S. Will Not Be Party to Russian Plan -- Ministers Agree on Final Sessions MOLOTOV BLOCKS AN AUSTRIAN PACT
stories.summary: Soviet Minister Molotov blocked an Austrian treaty by insisting on certain conditions, which the US considered fraudulent. The ministers agreed to hold a final session to discuss the treaty.

stories.headline: WEST SAID TO ASK SOVIET TO BID MAO DROP VIETMINH AID; Molotov's Answer Is Awaited -- French Hopes Gaining for Talks on Indo-China War WILSON VOICES OPTIMISM Asserts Victory Is 'Probable' -- Seeks to Dispel the Air of Crisis in Washington WEST REPORTED IN INDO-CHINA BID
stories.summary: The Western powers reportedly asked the Soviet Union to persuade Communist China to stop providing military aid to Vietminh. The outcome of this request is still awaited.

stories.headline: CITY REALTY VALUE RISES HALF BILLION TO A RECORD HIGH; $20,369,499,758 Is Set as Tentative Total, but May Be Cut Before Final Action CITY REALTY TOTAL UP HALF A BILLION
stories.summary: The total value of real estate in the city has increased by half a billion dollars to a record high of over 20 billion dollars.

rounds.blurb: Molotov blocked an Austrian treaty by insisting on certain conditions. The White House denied smearing the Democratic Party, saying it presented facts. The Western powers asked the Soviet Union to persuade China to stop aiding Vietminh.
